coredata.dc:title: Fluorometric detection of dopamine based on 3-aminophenylboronic acid-functionalized AgInZnS QDs and cells imaging
coredata.dc:creator: Liu, Yibing
He, Xu
Ma, Pinyi
Huang, Yibing
Li, Xiaolei
Sun, Ying
Wang, Xinghua
Song, Daqian
coredata.dc:description: Herein, cysteine capped AgInZnS QDs (Cys-AIZS QDs) with a large stoke shift and excellent biocompatibility were synthesized by a one-step aqueous method, followed by modified with 3-aminophenylboronic acid (APBA). Dopamine (DA) as an important neurotransmitter in brain can lead to significantly decrease in the fluorescence intensity of 3-aminophenylboronic acid-functionalized Cys-AIZS QDs (APBA-AIZS QDs) in a large concentration range of 1.5–900 μM. Good linearity can be obtained in the range of 15–120 μM, with a limit of detection (LOD) of 0.65 μM. Moreover, Cys-AIZS QDs and APBA-AIZS QDs were applied to living cells imaging, and Cys-AIZS QDs were applied to the co-localization with lysosomes, indicative of the feasibility of intracellular detection.
coredata.dcterms:subject: AgInZnS QDs
3-Aminophenylboronic acid
Dopamine
Cells imaging
Lysosomes co-localization

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Synthesis of Cys-AIZS QDs and APBA-AIZS QDs
Fluorometric determination of dopamine and real samples detection
Characterization of Cys-AIZS QDs and APBA-AIZS QDs
Sensing mechanism of APBA-AIZS QDs toward dopamine
Determination of dopamine in real samples
Interference study of the sensor for dopamine detection
Cell viability study and cell imaging of QDs

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: TEM images, high-resolution TEM (inset) images and the histogram of size distributions (inset) of 0.1 mg mL−1 Cys-AIZS QDs (A) and APBA-AIZS QDs (B). Fluorescence emission spectra of Cys-AIZS QDs and APBA-AIZS QDs at the excitation wavelength of 360 nm (C). FT-IR spectra of Cys-AIZS QDs, pure APBA and APBA-AIZS QDs (D).
Schematic illustration for the detection of dopamine.

Fluorescence spectra of APBA-AIZS QDs (0.06 mg mL−1) at different dopamine concentrations (1.5–900 μM) (A), fluorescence intensity change (F0/F) at different dopamine concentrations, the inset is the calibration curve toward dopamine (B).
Interference of 10 mM Na+, K+, Ca2+ and 15 mM AA, UA, Glu, Arg, Thr, Leu, Tyra, Try, 5-HT, Phe, His, Cys, Hcy, GSH, Epi for dopamine determination.
Cell viability of Cys-AIZS QDs using HepG2 cells as a model cell (A), Confocal fluorescence microscopic images of 0.1 mg mL−1 Cys-AIZS QDs (B) and APBA-AIZS QDs (C).

Fluorescence micrographs of the distributions of lysosomes (A, a) and Cys-AIZS QDs (0.1 mg mL−1) (B, b) in HepG2 cells and HeLa cells, Merged image where yellow represents the mixed fluorescence from the LysoTracker and QDs (C, c), Intensity profiles of LysoTracker and QDs (D, d, E, e), Intensity correlation plot of LysoTracker and QDs (F, f). (For interpretation of the references to color in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the Web version of this article.)
Determination of DA in human serum samples and DA hydrochloride injection samples (n = 3).

head.ce:title: Fluorometric detection of dopamine based on 3-aminophenylboronic acid-functionalized AgInZnS QDs and cells imaging

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering
Shandong Provincial Key Laboratory of Chemical Energy Storage and Novel Cell Technology

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Shandong Provincial Key Laboratory of Chemical Energy Storage and Novel Cell Technology, Liaocheng University, Liaocheng 252059, China.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Herein, cysteine capped AgInZnS QDs (Cys-AIZS QDs) with a large stoke shift and excellent biocompatibility were synthesized by a one-step aqueous method, followed by modified with 3-aminophenylboronic acid (APBA). Dopamine (DA) as an important neurotransmitter in brain can lead to significantly decrease in the fluorescence intensity of 3-aminophenylboronic acid-functionalized Cys-AIZS QDs (APBA-AIZS QDs) in a large concentration range of 1.5–900 μM. Good linearity can be obtained in the range of 15–120 μM, with a limit of detection (LOD) of 0.65 μM. Moreover, Cys-AIZS QDs and APBA-AIZS QDs were applied to living cells imaging, and Cys-AIZS QDs were applied to the co-localization with lysosomes, indicative of the feasibility of intracellular detection.

ce:section.ce:para: Dopamine (DA) is the most abundant catecholamine neurotransmitter in the brain, which regulates various physiological functions of the central nervous system [1–3]. The normal concentration of dopamine in human blood is 0.2–0.4 μg mL−1, dysregulation of the dopamine system can lead to Parkinson's disease, schizophrenia, attention deficit hyperactivity syndrome and pituitary tumors [4–6]. Therefore, dopamine is closely linked to human brain and physical health [7,8], and it is particularly important to establish a rapid and reliable method for detecting dopamine in biological fluids and drug samples, which is of great importance to health clinics and pharmaceutical industry. Up to now, several methods have been developed for the determination of dopamine, such as electrochemistry [9,10], colorimetric methods [11], chemiluminescence [12], high performance liquid chromatography [13], SERS [14,15] and fluorescent spectroscopy [16–20]. Compared with other methods, fluorescent spectroscopy has the advantages of simplicity, no time consuming, low cost and no need of complicated instruments.

Quantum dots (QDs) as a kind of semiconductor nanomaterials possess good luminescence performance, which are widely used in biosensing [21,22] and bioimaging [23,24]. Cd-based QDs and Pb-based QDs contain heavy metals Cd and Pb, which cannot be ignored for garbage recycling and environmental management, and also considered to be highly toxic and carcinogenic [25]. Therefore, nontoxicity QDs have become a hot research topic and it is significant to construct a probe with good luminescence performance and biocompatibility. Based on the above, I–III–VI (where I = Cu or Ag, III = Ga or In, VI = S or Se) based QDs such as AgInS2 and CuInS2 have aroused people intense attention, due to their low toxicity and environmental friendliness [26]. In particular, Zn-doped I–III–VI QDs exhibit better photoluminescence (PL) emission and show greater potential in detection applications [27]. Apart from optical properties, biotoxicity is also an important criterion to measure the performance of QDs. Surface capping ligand as an important factor will affect the performance of QDs, choosing a proper stabilizer is necessary. Cysteine (Cys) as a natural thiol-containing amino acid with low toxicity, is considered to be an ideal stabilizer with better biocompatibility [28]. Therefore, one-step aqueous synthesized cysteine capped AgInZnS QDs with excellent biocompatibility are applied to biological detection on account of their ideal optical properties.

In this paper, we synthesized biocompatible cysteine capped AgInZnS QDs (Cys-AIZS QDs) with a large stoke shift in aqueous phase via one step, then, Cys-AIZS QDs were functionalized by 3-aminophenylboronic acid (APBA) via EDC/NHS condensation reaction. Due to the specific identification of boric acid functional groups and vicinal diols, 3-aminophenylboronic acid-functionalized Cys-AIZS QDs (APBA-AIZS QDs) can be used to detect dopamine selectively. As dopamine levels increased, the fluorescence intensity of APBA-AIZS QDs decreased gradually. Thus, a cost-effective and straightforward sensor was constructed for the determination of dopamine. Furthermore, Cys-AIZS QDs were used for cells imaging and co-localization with lysosomes, and APBA-AIZS QDs can also be used for imaging living cells, which proved their feasibility of biological detection and clinical application.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synthesis of Cys-AIZS QDs and APBA-AIZS QDs
ce:section.ce:para: Cys-AIZS QDs using cysteine as stabilizer were fabricated in one step based on a previous paper with minor modification [29]. AgNO3 (0.05 M, 0.2 mL), Zn (ac)2·8H2O (0.05 M, 1 mL), In(ac)3 (0.05 M, 2 mL) and cysteine (0.02 M, 5 mL) were mixed in a 100 mL three-neck flask. Using 0.1 M NaOH solution to adjust the pH of the mixture to 9.5 and Na2S·9H2O (0.2 M, 2 mL) was added subsequently. The solution was heated to 100 °C in open air by using a condenser for 4.5 h. After cooling to ambient temperature, the reaction solution was centrifuged at 8000 rpm for 10 min. The supernatant was then precipitated using ethanol at room temperature and centrifuged for three times. Finally, the pure Cys-AIZS QDs were obtained by drying under vacuum.

APBA functionalized Cys-AIZS QDs were synthesized by the following method: 4 mg Cys-AIZS QDs were dissolved in 5 mL PBS solution (0.1 M, pH 7.4), then, 4 mg EDC and 4 mg NHS were added and reacted for 20 min to activate the carboxyl groups of QDs. Next, 2 mL APBA solution (1 mg mL−1) was added and stirred for 2 h before incubating at 4 °C overnight. Finally, the solution was centrifuged at 8000 rpm for 10 min to remove the unreacted APBA and dried under vacuum.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fluorometric determination of dopamine and real samples detection
ce:section.ce:para: For dopamine determination, 100 μL APBA-AIZS QDs (0.6 mg mL−1), 100 μL PBS solution (0.1 M, pH 7.4) and different concentrations of dopamine solution were added to 1 mL centrifuge tube. The mixture was diluted to 1 mL by ultrapure water and mixed uniformly for 15 min at room temperature. Then, the fluorescence spectra were recorded from 470 nm to 650 nm with an excitation wavelength of 360 nm. The photomultiplier tube voltage was set to 700 V, both the excitation and emission slit widths were set to 5 nm.

Human serum samples were collected from healthy adult volunteers by the First Hospital of Jilin University and approved by the ethics committee. For human serum samples determination, human serum was diluted to 100-fold by using PBS solution (0.1 M, pH 7.4) without further purification before detection. To obtain spiked human serum samples (30, 60, 90 μM), 3, 6, 9 μL dopamine (1 mM) was diluted to 100 μL with above human serum solution, and the detection method was the same as the above. For DA hydrochloride injection samples determination, DA hydrochloride injection was diluted to 200-fold with a final concentration of 26.35 μM. To obtain spiked DA hydrochloride injection samples (0, 20, 40 μM), 0, 2, 4 μL dopamine (1 mM) was diluted to 100 μL with above diluted DA hydrochloride injection solution and the detection method was the same as the above.

The cytotoxicity of Cys-AIZS QDs was evaluated by colorimetric procedure (standard MTT assay). HepG2 cells were treated with Cys-AIZS QDs of various concentrations (0.01, 0.02, 0.04, 0.06, 0.08, 0.1, 0.2 mg mL−1) and incubated at 37 °C for 24 h. Then, 20 μL of MTT solution (0.5 mg mL−1) in Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium (DMEM) was added after the removal of culture medium. After incubation for 4 h, 150 μL of DMSO was added and shaking for 10 min. Finally, the absorbance was recorded at 490 nm with a microplate reader.

For cell imaging and co-localization with lysosomes, HepG2 cells and HeLa cells were used. The cells were cultured in DMEM with 10% fetal bovine serum albumin (BSA) at 37 °C under 5% CO2. For living cells imaging, Cys-AIZS QDs (0.1 mg mL−1) and APBA-AIZS QDs (0.1 mg mL−1) were incubated with HepG2 cells for 3 h. For co-localization with lysosomes, 0.1 mg mL−1 Cys-AIZS QDs were added to the cells and incubated for 3 h, followed by addition of LysoTracker (lysosome-specific fluorescent dyes) for 30 min. The confocal fluorescent images of QDs were performed on a LSM 710 (CarlZeiss) confocal microscope.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Characterization of Cys-AIZS QDs and APBA-AIZS QDs
ce:section.ce:para: In this work, TEM, EDS, FTIR and fluorescence spectrometry were studied to characterize Cys-AIZS QDs and APBA-AIZS QDs. As displayed in Fig. 1 A, the synthesized Cys-AIZS QDs were spherical and had an average size of 4.25 nm. After functionalized by APBA, the average size of APBA-AIZS QDs were increased to 5.25 nm (Fig. 1B). Furthermore, the elementary compositions of the QDs were employed by energy-dispersive spectrometry (EDS). Fig. S1 showed that Cys-AIZS QDs were composed of Ag, In, Zn, S, C, N and O, the existence of C, N, O and S were attributed to the stabilizer cysteine. The presence of B in Fig. S2 illustrated that APBA was successfully modified on the surface of Cys-AIZS QDs. After APBA was linked to the QDs, no obvious change of excitation wavelength and maximum emission wavelength was observed, and the synthesized QDs possessed a large stoke shift nearly 180 nm (Fig. 1C). To further prove that APBA was modified on the surface of Cys-AIZS QDs, FTIR spectra of Cys-AIZS QDs, pure APBA and APBA-AIZS QDs was studied (Fig. 1D). The absorption peaks observed at 3000-3600 cm−1 of the three spectrums were attributed to the N–H/O–H stretching vibration. The peak appeared at 1040 cm−1 of Cys-AIZS QDs was corresponding to C–NH2 stretching vibration and the two carboxylates characteristic peaks of 1629 and 1401 cm−1 indicative of cysteine was bond as a carboxylate [30]. The four characteristic peaks located at 1384, 1154, 1078 and 996 cm−1 of APBA-AIZS QDs were attributed to B–O stretching vibration, B–O–H bending vibration, C–B stretching vibration and B–O–H deformation vibration, respectively [31,32]. Furthermore, the peak of 1560 cm−1 was assigned to amide groups (–CONH). These results demonstrated the successful combination between Cys-AIZS QDs and APBA.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Sensing mechanism of APBA-AIZS QDs toward dopamine
ce:section.ce:para: The surface of Cys-AIZS QDs contained carboxyl group due to the stabilizer cysteine, through EDC/NHS coupling reaction, Cys-AIZS QDs were functionalized by APBA. The abundant B–OH groups on the surface of QDs enabled the specific recognition between APBA-AIZS QDs and dopamine, due to the high affinity of boronate toward vicinal diols [33,34]. The added dopamine combined with QDs to form five-membered cyclic boronate ester [35], which might promote the electron transfer (ET) from APBA-AIZS QDs to dopamine [36]. Thus, the fluorescence intensity of APBA-AIZS QDs was gradually decreased (Fig. 2 ). UV–Vis absorption spectra of APBA-AIZS QDs interacting with dopamine were recorded (Fig. S3). The absorption peak was increased after adding dopamine, which was probably related to the combination of B–OH groups and vicinal diols. In addition, the changes in fluorescence were in accordance with the Stern-Volmer plot [36], indicative of the appearance of static quenching mechanism.

In order to achieve the optimal detection effect of this sensor, pH, reaction time and the mass ratio of Cys-AIZS QDs to APBA were studied. To obtain APBA-AIZS QDs, different ratios of Cys-AIZS QDs and APBA were investigated. Fig. S4 showed, the fluorescence intensity reached maximum when the mass ratio of QDs to APBA was 2:1, as a result, we used this ratio to synthesize APBA QDs. As shown in Fig. S5, in the pH range of 4.68–10.68, the quenching efficiency of dopamine to APBA-AIZS QDs increased gradually until pH 7.4 and then decreased, so we chose pH 7.4 for dopamine detection. With the increase of reaction time, the fluorescence intensity of the QDs gradually reduced and remained flat within 15 min (Fig. S6).

Quantitative detection of dopamine was performed under the optimal conditions. The fluorescence of the sensor was decreased gradually within a large dopamine concentration range of 1.5–900 μM (Fig. 3 A) and the limit of detection (LOD) was calculated to be 0.65 μM (S/N = 3). Good linearity between F0/F and dopamine concentrations within the range of 15–120 μM was obtained (Fig. 3B), the regression equation was F0/F = 0.0055 [Dopamine] + 1.225 with the r value of 0.9967. This was consistent with the Stern–Volmer equation F0/F = 1 + Ksv [Q], and the Stern–Volmer constant (Ksv) was 5.5 × 103 μM−1 approximately.

Table S1 showed the comparison of this sensor with several other methods. Except for what was listed here, some of methods had the detection limits as low as nM levels. However, an adult >17 years old has a typical normal range of 0.34–31.39 μM of dopamine [36]. Thus, this sensor was suitable for the detection of abnormally high levels dopamine, with the advantage of simple to synthesize and operate. Moreover, the detection time of this sensor was shorter than most of other methods and the detection range was broader with a satisfactory detection limit.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Determination of dopamine in real samples
ce:section.ce:para: In order to prove the practical application performance of the probe, human serum samples spiked with dopamine were detected. As listed in Table 1 , the recovery of dopamine was in the range of 113.1–96.00% with the relative standard deviation (RSD) values no more than 3%. In addition, this method was also applied to the analysis of dopamine in the samples of DA hydrochloride injection. The recovery was in the range of 97.10–104.5% with the RSD values no more than 4%. These results demonstrated the possibility of this probe for the detection of dopamine in real samples.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Interference study of the sensor for dopamine detection
ce:section.ce:para: Selectivity is one of the most important capabilities of a probe, therefore, we investigated the effects of some interfering substances, including 10 mM Na+, K+, Ca2+ and 15 mM ascorbic acid (AA), uric acid (UA), glucose (Glu), arginine (Arg), threonine (Thr), leucine (Leu), tyramine (Tyra), tryptamine (Try), serotonin hydrochloride (5-HT), phenethylamine (Phe), histamine (His), cysteine (Cys), homocysteine (Hcy), glutathione (GSH) and epinephrine (Epi). As shown in Fig. 4 , no obvious fluorescence changes were appeared except for epinephrine, which is due to the similar structure. However, the concentration of epinephrine in organisms is less than 300 pg mL−1, and the concentration of dopamine is 10-fold–100-fold higher, the influence of epinephrine is negligible [8]. These results demonstrated the selectivity and specificity of this probe for dopamine detection.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Cell viability study and cell imaging of QDs
ce:section.ce:para: The toxicity of QDs is the main factor affecting biological application, thus, the cellular cytotoxicity of Cys-AIZS QDs was determined by the MTT assay. As shown in Fig. 5 A, the viability of HepG2 cells was nearly 100% even at a high QDs concentration up to 0.8 mg mL−1, indicative of the non-toxic property of Cys-AIZS QDs. Compared to our previous work [37], Cys-AIZS QDs possessed more biocompatible than MPA-AIZS QDs, due to the capping agent cysteine which was a natural thiol-containing amino acid. Thus, Cys-AIZS QDs showed the advantages of being more suitable for biological detection. Fig. 5B and C exhibited the confocal fluorescence microscopic images of Cys-AIZS QDs and APBA-AIZS QDs with HepG2 cells after incubated for 3 h. The morphologies of the cells can be clearly observed, and strong fluorescence was present in the cytoplasm and weakened in the nuclei region. These results demonstrated QDs can enter the cytoplasm of living cells within 3 h.

Lysosomes are cellular organelles which can digest both endogenous and exogenous substances, and it can be found that QDs ultimately distributed in lysosomes by the confocal imaging [38]. The red fluorescence (Fig. 6 A, a) represented the distribution of lysosomes labeled by LysoTracker and Cys-AIZS QDs exhibited green fluorescence (Fig. 6B, b) in the cells. The yellow color (Fig. 6C, c) represented the merged images which indicated the co-localization of Cys-AIZS QDs with lysosomes. In addition, according to the intensity correlation diagram Fig. 6D, d, E, e and F, f, the Pearson's colocation coefficient of HepG2 cells and HeLa cells was calculated by software as 0.828 and 0.825, overlap coefficient was 0.998 and 0.992, respectively. These results exhibited that the fluorescence of LysoTracker and Cys-AIZS QDs can overlap very well, which proved the lysosomal targeting property of Cys-AIZS QDs. Thus, endocytosis was considered to be the main mechanism of QDs transmission in cells, and lysosomes were the destinations [39]. According to the above cells imaging, information about intracellular QDs delivery was provided, and the application of QDs for internal detection in lysosomes showed great feasibility.

To sum up, a fluorometric sensor for selective and effective detection of dopamine was constructed, and this probe was applied to serum and injection samples detection successfully. Cys-AIZS QDs with a large stoke shift of 180 nm were synthesized in aqueous phase via one step and functionalized by APBA via EDC/NHS condensation reaction. The fluorescence intensity of the probe was decreased gradually as dopamine levels increased in a wide range, due to the specific recognition between boronate and vicinal diols. This method was simple and no need for complicated processing. Furthermore, biocompatible Cys-AIZS QDs were applied to co-localization with lysosomes and APBA-AIZS QDs showed applicability in cellular imaging, indicative of the possibility of in vivo detection and bioimaging.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by Science and Technology Developing Foundation of Jilin Province of China (Nos. 20180201050YY and 201701011106JC) and Industrial Innovation Funds of Jilin Province of China (No. 2018C034-1).

ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.talanta.2020.121081.
